message:
fix(fp-frame): preserve Int64/Bool dtype in DataFrame.mode pad cells (br-frankenpandas-gwa2)

mode_output_dtype previously widened any column with a missing value
to Float64, even when the only missing values were padding cells
inserted to align unequal mode counts across columns. Pandas oracle
keeps the original dtype and stores a null marker — fp-conformance
FP-P2D-057/dataframe_mode_ties_strict was failing because column 'b'
became Float64(3.0) instead of Int64(3) after padding to match
column 'a' s tied modes.

Two coordinated changes:

- mode_output_dtype: separate padding-induced missingness from
  genuine float widening. saw_int + saw_missing now resolves to
  Int64 (and saw_bool + saw_missing to Bool); only saw_float or
  Bool+Int mixes still widen to Float64. All-null columns fall back
  to Float64 so Column::new can materialize a nullable numeric
  column.
- mode_axis1: pre-promote Int values to Float when any selected
  source column is Float64. Pandas' axis=1 numeric_only treats the
  per-row numeric slice as a single dtype-promoted Series; without
  this, an Int mode value next to NaN padding would now wrongly
  keep an axis=1 output column at Int64 across rows whose source
  was already Float.

3 new fp-frame tests cover Int64-padding preservation, Bool-padding
preservation, and the genuine-float widening guard. Conformance:
packet_filter_runs_dataframe_rank_mode_packet now passes;
df_mode_axis1_numeric_only_matches_pandas_example unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
